failure_surface: Agent completed workbook inspection but never implemented the required formula solutions or formatting changes. Test cases all failed (score 0). The agent printed workbook structure and saved it, but no actual modifications were made to cells E2:E7.
root_cause: Agent prematurely concluded the task after the information acquisition phase. The code only inspected and logged the spreadsheet state without implementing the dual requirement: (1) fixing the VLOOKUP formula to handle duplicate product lookups across multiple rows, and (2) applying the specified formatting (cell shading #FCE4D6, Calibri font, 0-as-blank display) to the answer position range E2:E7/E2:E9.
minimal_fix_hypothesis: After completing spreadsheet inspection, agents must explicitly verify whether the current cell contents already satisfy the task requirements before concluding. If formulas exist but do not meet specifications (e.g., VLOOKUP returns only first match when duplicates require alternative approach), or if formatting is missing where explicitly requested, the agent must generate and execute modification code before terminating.
evidence: Evaluation result shows all test cases failed (test_case_results: [0])
Solution code only contains workbook loading, sheet inspection, and cell printing - no formula modifications or styling
Conversation shows agent stated 'I'll implement the practical solution' but the actual code executed only exploration/printing logic
Inspection revealed existing VLOOKUP formulas in E2:E5 that return single matches despite task requiring multiple-match handling
Answer position E2:E7 requires shading, font, and blank-display modifications which were never applied
File was saved after inspection but remains in original unmodified state

memory_items.title: Complete Task Requirements After Inspection
memory_items.lesson: Workbook inspection is preparatory work, not task completion. All requested operations (formula changes, value updates, formatting, deletions) must be explicitly implemented in code after understanding the spreadsheet structure.
memory_items.generalization: Always verify that every operation mentioned in the task instruction has corresponding implementation code before concluding the task.

memory_items.title: Formula Validation Against Original Intent
memory_items.lesson: When existing formulas are found in target ranges, validate their logic against task requirements. A VLOOKUP returning single matches may not satisfy duplicate-handling requirements; do not assume existing formulas are correct.
memory_items.generalization: Check if pre-existing formulas align with the specific behavior requested (array functions vs scalar functions, multiple matches vs first match, etc.) before deciding whether to preserve or replace them.

memory_items.title: Explicit Formatting Must Have Corresponding Code
memory_items.lesson: Cell-level formatting instructions (fill color, font family, number format, 0-display behavior) require explicit openpyxl style application code. Printing inspection output does not fulfill formatting requirements.
memory_items.generalization: Documented formatting commands must map to PatternFill, Font, NumberFormat objects applied to the specified cell range via openpyxl before saving.

edits.content:
## 6. Execution Hygiene & Verification
- **Completeness Check**: After writing code, verify every task requirement has corresponding implementation (formulas, values, styles, formats).
- **Formula Logic Review**: When existing formulas are present, confirm they produce the exact behavior requested (multiple matches vs first match, dynamic vs static, etc.).
- **Formatting Implementation**: Cell appearance changes (fills, fonts, number formats, conditional formatting) require explicit library calls before save.
- **No Early Termination**: Do not conclude after successful inspection/save unless all requested modifications have been coded and verified.
